meta.setting: Saw camp in mixed conifer forest above French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in logging labor and resource pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Logging, ranching, mining, and resource-use context around the region.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California commerce and material texture.

image_prompt.subject: a timber boss at a saw camp shouting over a crosscut crew while logs wait on skids and oxen stand in mud
image_prompt.scene: cold morning in mixed conifer forest; sawdust haze, cook smoke, rough bunkhouse, dangerous slope beyond, and a ledger tucked under the boss's arm
image_prompt.composition: square busy worksite with boss offset, logs creating heavy horizontal lines, crew and oxen showing the cost of the command
image_prompt.camera: slightly low worksite angle with early-HD texture on bark, sawdust, and mud
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Let him be competent enough to be dangerous; the trees are not the only things being counted.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine logging worksite
render_traits: slightly simplified boss, crew, oxen, and log silhouettes
modest texture resolution on bark, sawdust, wool, mud, and leather
hard ambient occlusion under logs, oxen, saws, and skid timbers
cold morning light with sawdust haze
believable 1899 logging tools and camp clothing
resource-pressure composition rather than heroic lumber portrait
style_modifiers.palette: fresh pine gold, mud umber, ox brown, wool gray, sawdust tan, iron gray, cook-smoke blue, and conifer green.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable ledger entries, crate labels, signs, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The timber boss, crew, logs, oxen, saws, and muddy worksite are readable.
Every visible prop belongs plausibly to 1899 logging work.
The image shows labor pressure and extraction rather than nostalgic scenery.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
